body_html:
defintely

--- DKIESQ@aol.com wrote:

Should we not hold onto the check until the awol
employee eventually comes
back looking for his check and at that time have the
employee sign our basic
release letter? The employee left without any word.
Why are we mailing his
check to the last known address? What if someone
else picks up the check and
cashes it?

Jeannie is holding onto the pay check until I give
Jeannie instructions.
May I tell Jeannie to hold onto the check until Dale
Mark comes asking for it?

Darren K. Indyke
Attorney-At-Law
457 Madison Avenue
4th Floor
New York, New York 10022

Greetings
I have placed a termination letter on his file.
He will receive a letter addressed to his last know
address. I am waiting for the payroll to run as we
owe him two days pay. Once we have the cheque, I
will mail his letter. This will be on Monday.
He was absent on Monday 18 returned to work for
two days and then disappeared
Regards
Miles
